examples.title: Calculate Task Success Rate
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate task success rate from usability test data
examples.code:
INPUT: totalAttempts, successfulCompletions

FUNCTION CalculateTaskSuccessRate(totalAttempts, successfulCompletions):
 IF totalAttempts == 0 THEN
 RETURN 0
 END IF
 
 successRate = (successfulCompletions / totalAttempts) * 100
 RETURN successRate

// Example usage
totalUsers = 20
successfulUsers = 15
result = CalculateTaskSuccessRate(totalUsers, successfulUsers)
OUTPUT result // Returns 75%
examples.explanation: This pseudocode calculates the percentage of users who successfully completed a task during usability testing. A task success rate above 80% is typically considered good usability.

examples.title: Calculate Net Promoter Score
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate NPS from user survey responses
examples.code:
INPUT: ratings[] // Array of ratings 0-10 from users

FUNCTION CalculateNPS(ratings):
 promoters = 0
 detractors = 0
 total = LENGTH(ratings)
 
 FOR EACH rating IN ratings DO
 IF rating >= 9 THEN
 promoters = promoters + 1
 ELSE IF rating <= 6 THEN
 detractors = detractors + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Calculate percentages
 promoterPercent = (promoters / total) * 100
 detractorPercent = (detractors / total) * 100
 
 // NPS ranges from -100 to +100
 nps = promoterPercent - detractorPercent
 RETURN nps

// Example: ratings = [9, 10, 7, 8, 3, 2, 9, 5]
promoters = 4, detractors = 2
nps = 50 - 25 = 25
examples.explanation: NPS classifies users as Promoters (9-10), Passives (7-8), or Detractors (0-6). The score is the percentage of Promoters minus percentage of Detractors. A positive NPS above 50 is considered good.

examples.title: Calculate SUS Score
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate System Usability Scale score from survey responses
examples.code:
INPUT: responses[] // Array of 10 responses (1-5 scale)

FUNCTION CalculateSUS(responses):
 IF LENGTH(responses) != 10 THEN
 RETURN ERROR "SUS requires exactly 10 responses"
 END IF
 
 score = 0
 
 // Odd questions (1,3,5,7,9): score - 1
 // Even questions (2,4,6,8,10): 5 - score
 FOR i FROM 0 TO 9 DO
 IF (i % 2) == 0 THEN // Odd question (0-indexed)
 score = score + (responses[i] - 1)
 ELSE // Even question
 score = score + (5 - responses[i])
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Multiply by 2.5 to get 0-100 scale
 susScore = score * 2.5
 RETURN susScore

// Example: responses = [4,2,5,1,4,2,4,2,5,3]
// Calculated score * 2.5 = 76
// Result: 76 (Good usability)
examples.explanation: SUS uses 10 questions alternating between positive and negative framing. Odd questions contribute positively (response - 1), even questions contribute negatively (5 - response). Final score multiplied by 2.5 gives the 0-100 scale.
